system_prompt: You are an expert at extracting professional resources (codes, standards, precedents, and reference materials) from engineering-ethics cases using the ProEthica nine-component formalism and the professional-ethics literature (McLaren precedents; Davis & Frankel professional codes). A resource's canonical identity is its source kind. Follow the extraction methodology, matching rules, and output format given in the user message exactly, and return only the requested JSON (no prose, markdown, or code fences).

label.description: Resource class name (canonical short reusable source-kind form)

resource_category.description: Controlled source kind that drives the five-kind subClassOf typing: ethical_code, technical_standard, case_precedent, legal_resource, reference_material. EthicalCode is typed subClassOf Guideline subClassOf Resource; the other four subClassOf Resource. A routing input, not stored as a literal

matched_uri.description: URI of matched class (null if new)

matched_label.description: Label of matched class (null if new)

new_resource_classes.description: Resource classes extracted from case text (CandidateResourceClass)

identifier.description: Unique instance descriptor (e.g. 'NSPE Code of Ethics')

resource_class.description: Label of the resource class this individual instantiates

document_title.description: The title of the source document

topic.description: The subject the resource addresses (the declared proeth:topic property, not the otherAttribute overflow)

used_by.description: The case actor who relied on the resource (the Facts-section signal); resolves to the availableTo edge, not stored as a literal

cited_by.description: The agent who cited the resource as authority (the Discussion-section signal, commonly the NSPE Board); resolves to the citedByAgent edge, not stored as a literal

provision_codes.description: For a code or regulation, the provision code(s) cited (e.g. 'I.1', 'II.2.b'); resolve to refersToDocument nspe# IRIs. Empty for a non-code resource

resource_individuals.description: Specific documents used or cited in the case (ResourceIndividual)

existing_resources_text.description: Formatted list of existing resource classes from ontology (plus the reuse-bias guidance block)

cross_concept_context.description: Resources are extracted in Step 1 and carry no cross-concept dependency, so this slot is empty for resources; retained for template uniformity

resource_definition.source: OntServe proethica-core.ttl core:Resource iao:0000115 + iao:0000116 via concept_ontology_slots -> _component_definition_block('Resource')
resource_definition.description: Governing definitional anchor: the ontology iao:0000115 definition plus the iao:0000116 extraction framing, citation-trimmed at injection (the generalized Role definition pattern)

resource_schema.source: OntServe SHACL shapes (validation/shapes/core-shapes.ttl) ResourceDefinitionShape via concept_ontology_slots -> _component_schema_block('Resource')
resource_schema.description: Resource definitional/individual field schema, resolved live from the SHACL ResourceDefinitionShape (single source shared with the OntServe Resource entity page). resource_category drives the five-kind subClassOf typing; document_title and topic are literals; used_by and cited_by resolve to the availableTo and citedByAgent edges

pass_directive.description: Per-pass directive; one body serves facts+discussion, differing only by this runtime slot (used_by is the Facts signal, cited_by the Discussion signal)

facts.name: Resources (facts)
facts.description: Extract new resource classes and resource individuals from the case facts (the codes, standards, and precedents the parties relied on; the used_by signal)

discussion.name: Resources (discussion)
discussion.description: Extract new resource classes and resource individuals from the case discussion, reusing the facts-pass resources via match_decision (the cited_by authority signal)
